title: cistus umbellatus subsp. viscosus
intro: Cistus umbellatus subsp. viscosus, also known as Rock Rose, is a perennial shrub native to the Mediterranean region and other parts of the world, characterized by its fragrant white flowers and sticky or glandular leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infection. The health benefits of Rock Rose include its ability to soothe skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to alleviate respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. Rock Rose can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed internally or applied topically to harness its medicinal properties.
